purpose: Craft examples for romance, reunion, misunderstanding, status pressure, contract marriage, emotional reversal, and relationship-driven short drama.
source_priority_rules: Relationship status, history, promises, betrayal, contract terms, and family pressure must come from source or locked payload.
Emotional turns should be expressed through behavior, choice, restraint, or public/private status change.
Do not add love triangles, pregnancy, marriage contracts, illness, or betrayal unless source supports them.
genre_boundary: Track who knows the emotional truth and who performs a public role.
Use status, timing, misunderstanding, sacrifice, or withheld information as pressure.
Keep dialogue concise and playable; subtext should not erase clear scene stakes.

scene_card_examples.visual: In the locked location, one character notices a source-grounded object or gesture that proves the other character has been hiding pressure.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The detail does not confess love; it proves what was never said out loud.

scene_card_examples.visual: A public setting forces one character to choose between protecting status and protecting the other person.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The choice matters because everyone is watching.

script_scene_examples.visual: One character starts to leave, then stops because a source-grounded line, object, or injury changes the meaning of the conflict.
script_scene_examples.voiceover: The misunderstanding does not end; it becomes harder to ignore.

dialogue.speaker: lead
dialogue.line: You knew, and you still came.

script_scene_examples.action_result: The relationship pressure shifts from accusation to unresolved care.

dialogue_style_examples: Romance dialogue should carry status, hurt, restraint, or choice.
Use subtext, but make the action result clear.
Do not make every line a confession; let behavior reveal pressure.

negative_examples.bad: Add a new ex-lover to create jealousy.
negative_examples.why_bad: Unsupported relationship additions damage source fidelity.

negative_examples.bad: Resolve a deep misunderstanding with one generic apology.
negative_examples.why_bad: It collapses emotional causality and weakens episode hooks.

forbidden_drift: Do not add marriage, pregnancy, illness, betrayal, contract, family opposition, or love triangle facts unless source-grounded.
Do not change who loves whom, who knows what, or who hurt whom without source support.
Do not make characters confess information they are not ready or able to reveal according to the locked scene.

image: Lock two-person distance, eye line, public/private setting, and source-grounded object details.
video: Use reaction shots, hand pauses, blocked exits, and public gaze as emotional continuity anchors.
